reasoning: Patch derived from muruvetg/from-simulation-goals-to-parameters output for Sepsis. The SIMOD baseline shows all activity durations as near-zero (instantaneous processing) — all delays are captured as resource queue waiting times. Therefore only resource count increases can meaningfully reduce waiting times. Suggestion 1 (fast-track registration via dedicated staff) maps to adding a resource to profile A which covers ER Registration and IV Antibiotics. Suggestion 2 (priority lab processing) maps to adding a resource to profile B which covers CRP, Leucocytes, and LacticAcid. Suggestions 3,4,5,6,7,8 involve cross-training policies, automated systems, scheduling protocols, communication tools, and monitoring dashboards — none map to Prosimos parameters.

modifications.rationale: Suggestion 1 targets a dedicated fast-track registration system with dedicated staff for sepsis cases. Adding one resource to profile A (which covers ER Registration, ER Sepsis Triage, IV Antibiotics, IV Liquid) directly reduces queue waiting time at the entry point of the sepsis pathway.
modifications.evidence_source: Base repo suggestion 1: implement fast-track registration system with dedicated staff for suspected sepsis cases
modifications.feasibility_assumptions: One additional staff member can be dedicated to sepsis-suspect cases as suggested by the base system
modifications.intervention: Add one resource to A_profile covering ER Registration and IV Antibiotics
modifications.mechanism_rationale: Doubling capacity at the critical entry and treatment activities halves the expected queue waiting time under steady-state conditions

modifications.rationale: Suggestion 2 targets a priority queue for lab tests (Leucocytes, CRP, Lactic Acid). Profile B covers these three lab activities. Adding one resource models a dedicated fast-track lab slot for sepsis-suspect samples.
modifications.evidence_source: Base repo suggestion 2: establish priority queue for processing sepsis-related blood tests (Leucocytes, CRP, Lactic Acid)
modifications.feasibility_assumptions: One additional lab resource can be dedicated to sepsis-suspect samples as suggested
modifications.intervention: Add one resource to B_profile covering CRP, Leucocytes, and LacticAcid
modifications.mechanism_rationale: Additional lab capacity reduces queue waiting time for diagnostic tests, shortening the time from arrival to antibiotic administration

expected_kpi_impacts.reasoning: Doubling capacity at ER Registration reduces queue waiting time substantially
Faster registration and lab processing shorten the critical path to IV Antibiotics

expected_kpi_impacts.kpi_name: Lab Turnaround Time by Test Type
expected_kpi_impacts.reasoning: Doubled lab capacity directly reduces waiting time for CRP, Leucocytes, and LacticAcid

unresolved_kpis.kpi_name: Wait Before Admission by Org Group
unresolved_kpis.explanation: Suggestions 3-8 address cross-training, automated reporting, task prioritisation, scheduling, communication systems, and dashboards — none map to Prosimos resource or duration parameters

warnings: All SIMOD activity durations for sepsis are fixed at 0.0 seconds — delays are entirely captured as resource queue waiting times. Duration-based modifications would have no effect. Only resource count changes are meaningful.

Suggestions 3 (cross-training), 4 (automated results reporting), 5 (task prioritisation protocol), 6 (shift scheduling), 7 (communication systems), and 8 (real-time monitoring) are operational/IT interventions with no direct Prosimos parameter equivalent.
